sections.markdown:
# CL-PPCRE: Perl-Compatible Regex

Common Lisp has no built-in regex. CL-PPCRE ("Portable Perl Compatible Regular Expressions") is the standard answer. It's pure Lisp, fast, and supports the full Perl 5 syntax.

The core surface:

* `(cl-ppcre:scan REGEX STRING)` — return four values: match-start, match-end, register-starts vector, register-ends vector. Returns `NIL` on no match.
* `(cl-ppcre:scan-to-strings REGEX STRING)` — high-level: returns the whole match as a string and a vector of capture-group strings.
* `(cl-ppcre:all-matches-as-strings REGEX STRING)` — list of every non-overlapping match.
* `(cl-ppcre:split REGEX STRING)` — split on regex.
* `(cl-ppcre:regex-replace REGEX STRING REPLACEMENT)` — replace first match.
* `(cl-ppcre:regex-replace-all REGEX STRING REPLACEMENT)` — replace every match.

The `REPLACEMENT` argument can be a string (with `\1`, `\2` for back-references) or a function called with the matched portion.

sections.explain: Find every run of digits. Note the doubled backslash in the string literal: \\d in source is \d in the regex.
Split on a comma followed by any whitespace. SPLIT returns a list of substrings.
Collapse runs of whitespace to underscores.

sections.code: (multiple-value-bind (whole groups) (cl-ppcre:scan-to-strings "(\\w+)@(\\w+)" "a@b foo bar") (list whole (aref groups 0) (aref groups 1)))
sections.explain: SCAN-TO-STRINGS returns the whole match and a vector of capture groups. We pull both out.

sections.prompt: Define COUNT-DIGITS (S) returning the number of digit-runs in S using CL-PPCRE:ALL-MATCHES-AS-STRINGS.
